fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Front Driveway; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area
fr.considerations: Dense Vegetation; Limited Rear Access; Single Access Point
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency responder effectiveness could be achieved by addressing current access and movement constraints on the property. Trimming or managing dense vegetation near the driveway and parking areas may assist in improving visibility and accessibility for responders. Creating a secondary access point could facilitate smoother ingress and egress, reducing congestion and enabling quicker response times. Additionally, ensuring a clear, wide path to the rear of the main building could provide critical access for emergency operations around multiple sides of the structure. Clearly marking and maintaining these routes for easy identification may further support effective navigation and staging activities for responders arriving on the scene.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Dense Tree Canopy; Vegetated Perimeter; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Tree Cluster; Vegetation Overhang; Mixed Vegetation Cover
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by increasing the separation between vegetation and the structure and reducing the density of vegetation adjacent to it could be beneficial. Trimming or removing overhanging branches, especially those that extend over the roof or near the access route, may reduce fuel continuity and support safer evacuation and firefighting access. Thinning the dense tree canopy and managing the clustered trees by selectively removing some may further decrease fuel accumulation while retaining canopy gaps. Maintaining clear, open areas along the perimeter of the property and addressing the mixed vegetation cover could support greater wildfire resilience, providing more effective barriers to fire spread. Additionally, reducing vegetation concentrations near the access route could improve firefighter defensibility and support suppression efforts.
